label: Jira
description: Connect to Jira to read, create, and update issues and projects in your Atlassian workspace.

form.title: Connect Jira
form.subtitle: Authenticate with your Atlassian account to access Jira issues and projects.

methods.label: API Token
methods.description: Paste your Atlassian API token along with your login email and Jira subdomain. Quickest path — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with an Atlassian API Token

Atlassian API tokens are tied to your user account and used in place of your password for HTTP Basic Auth (`email:token`).

### Steps

1. Log in to your Atlassian account and open [API tokens](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens).
2. Click **Create API token**.
3. Give it a descriptive label like `Anton` and click **Create**.
4. Copy the token from the dialog — it is only shown once.
5. Paste it into **API Token** below.
6. Enter your **Atlassian account email** (the one you log into Jira with).
7. Enter your **Jira subdomain** — the part before `.atlassian.net`, e.g. `yourorg` from `yourorg.atlassian.net`.

### Notes

- The token is used as the password in HTTP Basic Auth; your email is the username.
- Tokens do not expire by default, but a Workspace admin can revoke them at any time.
- If you lose the token, delete it in the API tokens page and create a new one.
- This method works with Jira Cloud. For Jira Server/Data Center on-premise, use username + password or a Personal Access Token instead.

fields.name: subdomain
fields.label: Jira Subdomain
fields.description: The subdomain part of your Jira URL — e.g. 'yourorg' from 'yourorg.atlassian.net'.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Atlassian Account Email
fields.description: The email address you use to log in to Jira. Used as the username in Basic Auth.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: The API token generated at id.atlassian.com → Security → API tokens. Shown only once at creation.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (3-LO)
methods.description: Authorize Anton via Atlassian's OAuth 2.0 flow using your own Atlassian app credentials. More involved to set up but does not require copying an API token.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Atlassian OAuth 2.0 (3-LO)

You must create an Atlassian OAuth app in the Atlassian Developer Console to get a Client ID and Secret.

### Steps

1. Go to the [Atlassian Developer Console](https://developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps/) and click **Create** → **OAuth 2.0 integration**.
2. Give it a name like `Anton` and agree to the terms.
3. On the app page, go to **Permissions** → add **Jira API** → select `read:jira-work`, `write:jira-work`, and `read:jira-user`.
4. Go to **Authorization** → **OAuth 2.0 (3LO)** → set the **Callback URL** to `http://localhost` (Anton handles the local redirect).
5. Go to **Settings** → copy the **Client ID** and **Secret**.
6. Paste them into the fields below and click **Submit**.
7. A browser window will open for you to authorize Anton against your Atlassian account.

### Notes

- The `offline_access` scope is required so Anton can refresh tokens without re-prompting you.
- After authorization, Anton uses the Atlassian REST API at `api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/{cloudId}`.
- You can revoke access at any time from [Atlassian Connected Apps](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/apps).

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From your Atlassian Developer Console app → Settings → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Found in the same Settings page in the Atlassian Developer Console.
